title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: March 29, 2026 - Weekend Update (Data as of March 27, 2026 Market Close)

items.headline: Wall Street Closes Fifth Consecutive Losing Week Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Surging Oil Prices
items.summary: U.S. stocks concluded their worst week since the Iran war began, marking their fifth straight week of losses. All major indices, including the S&P 500, Dow Jones Industrial Average, and Nasdaq Composite, finished in negative territory, driven by ongoing geopolitical uncertainty and a sharp rise in crude oil prices. The Nasdaq and Dow have now entered correction territory. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Drive Market Downturn; Oil Surges Above $110
items.summary: Wall Street's continued decline is largely attributed to investor caution surrounding President Trump's 10-day pause on Iran energy strikes and ongoing U.S.-Iran discussions. The S&P 500 completed its fifth straight week of losses, marking its poorest performance since the onset of the Iran war. This elevated uncertainty, particularly from stalled U.S.-Iran diplomacy, pushed crude oil prices above $100 per barrel, with Brent crude jumping over $110, triggering a broad market sell-off.

items.headline: Energy Sector Leads Gains Amidst Broader Market Weakness; Tech and Consumer Discretionary Lag
items.summary: In a week marked by broad market declines, the energy sector emerged as a significant outperformer, leading gains with an 18% month-to-date increase. This surge coincided with rising oil prices. Conversely, sectors such as Communication Services, Information Technology, and Industrials experienced substantial declines. The Consumer Discretionary sector also faced a challenging month, falling 12% due to concerns over gasoline prices and a dip in consumer sentiment. Defense stocks, including RTX and Lockheed Martin, saw significant gains, rising 22.1% and 19.4% month-to-date, respectively.

items.headline: Jobless Claims Tick Up, While Fed Holds Rates Steady
items.summary: Initial jobless claims for the week ending March 21 saw a slight increase of 5,000, reaching 210,000. However, continuing claims for the week ending March 14 decreased by 32,000 to 1,819,000, hitting its lowest point since October 2024. The Federal Reserve maintained its target interest rate at 3.50% - 3.75%, citing persistent uncertainty from the Iran conflict and stubborn inflation.

items.headline: Carnival, Paul Mueller, and AllianceBernstein Among Companies Reporting Earnings
items.summary: On March 27, Carnival ($CCL) was among several major companies expected to report earnings. Other companies, including Kopin ($KOPN) and Autolus Therapeutics ($AUTL), were also slated for earnings releases. Additionally, AllianceBernstein National Municipal Income Fund, Inc. ($AFB) announced its first fiscal quarter earnings, and Paul Mueller ($MUEL) also released its earnings report.

items.headline: Concerns Over 'AI Bubble' Lead to Investor Rotation Out of Tech Giants
items.summary: Investors are increasingly shifting away from large-cap growth and AI hyperscaler stocks due to growing concerns about an 'AI bubble,' contributing to a fear-driven market slide. Analysts highlight that over 50% of the U.S. GDP growth last year stemmed from AI Capital Expenditure, suggesting significant vulnerability if AI spending slows. This sentiment indicates a broader de-risking trend in the market.
